operating_ranges.evidence: Maximum continuous duty temperature, °C (°F) (in clean air) up to 1,400 (2,550) for F-14 grade
Classification temp. °C (°F) up to 1,600 (2,910) for F-14 grade
Density approx. kg/m³ (lb/ft³) ranges from 180-200 (11.24-12.48) across different grades
At 200°C (390°F): thermal conductivity ranges from 0.07 to 0.08 W/m.K
At 1,000°C (1,830°F): thermal conductivity ranges from 0.24 to 0.29 W/m.K

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Linear shrinkage, % (24 hours at max. continuous duty temperature) ranges from 3% to 4.5%
Care is necessary when integrating heating elements due to the potential for corrosion

fault_conditions.name: reduced_temperature_capability_hydrogen
fault_conditions.description: Maximum continuous duty temperature reduced in hydrogen atmosphere
fault_conditions.evidence: The maximum continuous duty temperature is reduced to 1,000°C (1,830°F) furnace temperature in a hydrogen atmosphere

fault_conditions.name: radiant_heat_loss_through_gaps
fault_conditions.description: Heat loss through gaps between modules due to tolerances and shrinkage
fault_conditions.evidence: To account for furnace, module tolerances, and module shrinkage, and to prevent radiant losses through module gaps

maintenance_actions.name: joint_sealing_with_ceramic_felt
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: prevents radiant heat losses through gaps
maintenance_actions.evidence: we recommend placing a double-folded layer of ceramic fiber felt between Fibrothal® modules

maintenance_actions.name: tig_welding_of_heating_elements
maintenance_actions.evidence: We recommend using the TIG (Tungsten Inert Gas) welding method if welding is necessary, particularly between the terminal and the heating element

assumptions.statement: Small furnace installations require no special mounting measures
assumptions.reason: For relatively small furnaces, such as tube furnaces with RAC modules, Fibrothal® half-cylinders or third cylinders, and muffle or chamber furnaces with Fibrothal® panels, no special mounting or fixing measures are typically required

assumptions.statement: Ceramic fiber felt must extend 25mm beyond module front
assumptions.reason: The ceramic fiber felt should extend at least 25 mm (0.98 in) beyond the front of the module, to reduce gaps between modules
